---
title: "Day 4 (Thursday): Models, Preprocessing, Metrics, and Importance"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, ../assets/css/slides-theme.scss]
    slide-number: true
    incremental: false
    scrollable: true
    slide-level: 2
    html-math-method: katex
    footer: '<a href="../index.html" style="text-decoration:underline;">Teaching Hub</a> · <a href="../exercises/index.html" style="text-decoration:underline;">Lab exercises</a> · <a href="?print-pdf" style="text-decoration:underline;">Print PDF</a>'
    output-file: day-04-thursday.html
    embed-resources: true
---

## Learning objectives

- Swap **`parsnip` specs** (bagging, boosting, neural nets) on the **same recipe + workflow** from Tuesday
- Compare **many models** fairly with **`fit_resamples()`** using **accuracy** until imbalance
- Add **dimension reduction**, **imputation**, and **resampling** as **recipe steps** inside **cross-validation (CV)**
- Choose **metrics beyond accuracy** (sensitivity, precision-recall, confusion matrices) for imbalanced data
- Read **variable importance** without causal over-interpretation

## Navigation

- [Teaching Hub](../index.qmd)
- [Curriculum Outline](../curriculum-outline.qmd)
- [Module 05  -  Advanced models](../modules/module-05-advanced-models.qmd)
- [Module 08  -  Compare learners](../modules/module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd)
- [Back: Day 2 (Tuesday) deck](day-02-tuesday.qmd)

## Terms used today (abbreviations)

| Term | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **CV** | **Cross-validation**  -  resample data into folds; train on some rows, evaluate on held-out rows |
| **PCA** | **Principal component analysis**  -  linear combinations of correlated predictors |
| **NA** | **Missing** value (not recorded) |
| **ROC** | **Receiver operating characteristic** curve  -  tradeoff of true vs false positives across thresholds |
| **PR** | **Precision-recall** curve  -  precision vs recall across thresholds |
| **AUC** | **Area under the curve**  -  one-number summary of a threshold curve (e.g. ROC-AUC, PR-AUC) |
| **VIP** | **Variable importance**  -  which predictors the model used most in splits |
| **MLP** | **Multilayer perceptron**  -  small feed-forward neural network |
| **SHAP** | **SHapley additive explanations**  -  local "why this prediction?" scores |
| **RF** | **Random forest**  -  bagged ensemble of decision trees |
| **GBM** | **Gradient boosting machine**  -  sequential trees that correct prior errors |
| **XGBoost** | **Extreme gradient boosting**  -  popular **GBM** engine in `parsnip` |
| **SVM** | **Support vector machine**  -  margin-based classifier (`svm_rbf()` uses a radial kernel) |
| **TP / FP / FN / TN** | **True/false positive/negative**  -  counts from a confusion matrix (Part E) |

# Part A  -  Recap (Tuesday's pipeline)

## Palmer penguins  -  back again

![](../assets/figures/shared/penguins/lter_penguins.png){width=72% fig-align="center"}

Same **`peng_bal`** table and **`rec_base`** recipe as Tuesday  -  today we swap models and add recipe steps.

## What you already have

- **`rec_base`**  -  `step_zv` (drop **zero-variance** columns) → `step_dummy` (one-hot categoricals) → `step_normalize` (scale numerics) on **`peng_bal`**
- **`workflow()`** + **`decision_tree` / `rpart`** tuned on Tuesday
- **`vfold_cv(..., strata = y)`**  -  **5-fold cross-validation (CV)** with both species in each fold
- **`metric_set(accuracy)`** for Parts B-D today (more metrics in Part E)

```{r}
#| label: day04-setup
#| include: false
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidymodels)
  library(palmerpenguins)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(rlang)
  library(vip)
  library(purrr)
  library(themis)
})
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center",
  out.width = "100%",
  dpi = 120
)
source("../R/slide-viz-helpers.R")

peng_cls <- palmerpenguins::penguins |>
  filter(species %in% c("Adelie", "Gentoo")) |>
  mutate(
    y = factor(species, levels = c("Adelie", "Gentoo")),
    year = as.numeric(year)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(-species, -flipper_length_mm, -body_mass_g) |>
  tidyr::drop_na()

folds <- vfold_cv(peng_cls, v = 5, strata = y)
metrics_acc <- metric_set(accuracy)

peng_bal <- peng_cls

rec_base <- recipe(y ~ ., data = peng_bal) |>
  step_zv(all_predictors()) |>
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) |>
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors())

tree_spec <- decision_tree(tree_depth = 4, min_n = 10) |>
  set_engine("rpart") |>
  set_mode("classification")

wf_tree <- workflow() |>
  add_recipe(rec_base) |>
  add_model(tree_spec)

set.seed(6)
rs_tree <- fit_resamples(wf_tree, folds, metrics = metrics_acc)

# Teaching scenarios
set.seed(4)
peng_imb <- peng_bal |>
  group_by(y) |>
  filter(y == "Adelie" | (y == "Gentoo" & dplyr::row_number() <= 10L)) |>
  ungroup()

peng_imb3 <- prep_penguins_multiclass_imbalance(
  minority_class = "Chinstrap",
  hard_to_class = "Adelie",
  n_minority = 15L
)

peng_na <- peng_bal
bill_length_true <- peng_na$bill_length_mm
set.seed(4)
na_bill <- sample(which(!is.na(peng_na$bill_length_mm)), size = 22L)
peng_na$bill_length_mm[na_bill] <- NA
na_sex <- sample(which(!is.na(peng_na$sex)), size = 5L)
peng_na$sex[na_sex] <- NA

peng_pca <- palmerpenguins::penguins |>
  filter(species %in% c("Adelie", "Gentoo")) |>
  mutate(
    y = factor(species, levels = c("Adelie", "Gentoo")),
    year = as.numeric(year)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(-species) |>
  tidyr::drop_na()

folds_imb <- vfold_cv(peng_imb, v = 5, strata = y)
folds_imb3 <- vfold_cv(peng_imb3, v = 5, strata = y3)
folds_pca <- vfold_cv(peng_pca, v = 5, strata = y)
# Gentoo = positive class for sens/spec (yardstick default = first level = Adelie)
sens_gentoo <- yardstick::metric_tweak("sens", sens, event_level = "second")
spec_gentoo <- yardstick::metric_tweak("spec", spec, event_level = "second")
roc_auc_gentoo <- yardstick::metric_tweak("roc_auc", roc_auc, event_level = "second")
pr_auc_gentoo <- yardstick::metric_tweak("pr_auc", pr_auc, event_level = "second")
metrics_cls_imb <- metric_set(
  accuracy,
  sens_gentoo,
  spec_gentoo,
  roc_auc_gentoo,
  pr_auc_gentoo
)

recall_macro <- yardstick::metric_tweak("recall_macro", sens, estimator = "macro")
f_meas_macro <- yardstick::metric_tweak("f_meas_macro", f_meas, estimator = "macro")
metrics_cls_imb3 <- metric_set(
  accuracy,
  recall_macro,
  f_meas_macro
)

plot_class_balance <- function(df, title = "Class counts") {
  ggplot(df, aes(y, fill = y)) +
    geom_bar(show.legend = FALSE) +
    geom_text(stat = "count", aes(label = after_stat(count)), vjust = -0.3, size = 4) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(Adelie = "turquoise", Gentoo = "magenta")) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = title, x = "Species (y)", y = "n")
}

plot_na_highlight <- function(df, xvar, yvar, title = NULL, margin_frac = 0.16) {
  x_obs <- df[[xvar]]
  x_rng <- range(x_obs, na.rm = TRUE)
  x_pad <- diff(x_rng) * margin_frac
  x_stub <- x_rng[1] - x_pad

  df <- df |>
    mutate(
      missing_x = is.na(.data[[xvar]]),
      x_plot = ifelse(missing_x, x_stub, .data[[xvar]])
    )
  miss <- dplyr::filter(df, missing_x)
  ok <- dplyr::filter(df, !missing_x)

  ggplot(df, aes(x = x_plot, y = .data[[yvar]])) +
    annotate(
      "rect",
      xmin = x_stub - x_pad * 0.55,
      xmax = x_rng[1] - x_pad * 0.05,
      ymin = -Inf,
      ymax = Inf,
      fill = "#FFEBEE",
      alpha = 0.45
    ) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = x_rng[1], linetype = "dotted", color = "gray55") +
    geom_point(
      data = ok,
      aes(color = y),
      size = 2.8,
      alpha = 0.85
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = miss,
      aes(x = x_plot, y = .data[[yvar]]),
      shape = 1,
      color = "#B71C1C",
      size = 12,
      stroke = 2.2,
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = miss,
      aes(x = x_plot, y = .data[[yvar]]),
      shape = 4,
      color = "#B71C1C",
      size = 5.5,
      stroke = 2.2,
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      limits = c(x_stub - x_pad * 0.5, x_rng[2] + x_pad * 0.1),
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(Adelie = "black", Gentoo = "gray40"), name = "Species") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = xvar,
      y = yvar,
      caption = "Pink band: bill length unknown (NA). Red × markers are NOT placed at the eventual imputed value."
    )
}

plot_imputed_highlight <- function(
    raw,
    filled,
    xvar,
    yvar,
    title = NULL,
    true_xvar = NULL,
    margin_frac = 0.16
) {
  stopifnot(nrow(raw) == nrow(filled))

  was_missing <- is.na(raw[[xvar]])
  idx <- which(was_missing)
  has_true <- !is.null(true_xvar) && true_xvar %in% names(raw)

  ok_df <- data.frame(
    x = filled[[xvar]][!was_missing],
    y = raw[[yvar]][!was_missing],
    species = raw$y[!was_missing],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  imp_df <- data.frame(
    x_imputed = filled[[xvar]][idx],
    y = raw[[yvar]][idx],
    species = raw$y[idx],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  if (has_true) {
    imp_df$x_true <- raw[[true_xvar]][idx]
  }

  p <- ggplot2::ggplot() +
    ggplot2::geom_point(
      data = ok_df,
      ggplot2::aes(x = x, y = y),
      color = "gray80",
      size = 2.4,
      alpha = 0.7
    )

  if (has_true) {
    p <- p +
      ggplot2::geom_segment(
        data = imp_df,
        ggplot2::aes(x = x_true, xend = x_imputed, y = y, yend = y),
        color = "#E65100",
        linewidth = 0.9,
        linetype = "dashed",
        arrow = grid::arrow(length = grid::unit(0.12, "inches"), type = "closed")
      ) +
      ggplot2::geom_point(
        data = imp_df,
        ggplot2::aes(x = x_true, y = y),
        shape = 1,
        color = "#37474F",
        size = 4.5,
        stroke = 1.4
      )
  }

  p +
    ggplot2::geom_point(
      data = imp_df,
      ggplot2::aes(x = x_imputed, y = y),
      shape = 21,
      fill = "#FFC107",
      color = "#B71C1C",
      size = 5.5,
      stroke = 2
    ) +
    ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(expand = ggplot2::expansion(mult = c(0.06, 0.04))) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = title,
      x = xvar,
      y = yvar,
      caption = if (has_true) {
        paste(
          "Open circles: true bill length (simulation only).",
          "Gold: median-imputed value for that same penguin.",
          "Gray background: birds with observed bill length."
        )
      } else {
        "Gold: median-imputed bill length for penguins that were missing on input."
      }
    )
}

metrics_summary_table <- function(fit_res, label) {
  collect_metrics(fit_res) |>
    dplyr::filter(.metric %in% c(
      "accuracy", "sens_gentoo", "spec_gentoo", "roc_auc_gentoo", "pr_auc_gentoo"
    )) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      .metric = dplyr::recode(
        .metric,
        sens_gentoo = "sens (Gentoo+)",
        spec_gentoo = "spec (Gentoo+)",
        roc_auc_gentoo = "roc_auc",
        pr_auc_gentoo = "pr_auc"
      )
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(.metric, mean, std_err) |>
    dplyr::mutate(model = label) |>
    dplyr::relocate(model)
}

compare_metrics_tbl <- function(...) {
  tab <- purrr::imap_dfr(rlang::list2(...), ~ metrics_summary_table(.x, .y)) |>
    dplyr::select(model, .metric, mean, std_err) |>
    dplyr::mutate(cell = sprintf("%.3f (%.3f)", mean, std_err)) |>
    dplyr::select(model, .metric, cell) |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = model, values_from = cell)
  knitr::kable(tab, caption = "Cross-validated metrics: mean (std err)")
}

plot_prob_boundary <- function(
    model,
    data,
    f1 = "bill_length_mm",
    f2 = "bill_depth_mm",
    title = NULL
) {
  r1 <- range(data[[f1]], na.rm = TRUE)
  r2 <- range(data[[f2]], na.rm = TRUE)
  grid <- expand.grid(
    seq(r1[1] - 0.5, r1[2] + 0.5, length.out = 120),
    seq(r2[1] - 0.5, r2[2] + 0.5, length.out = 120)
  )
  names(grid) <- c(f1, f2)
  for (nm in setdiff(names(data), c(f1, f2, "y"))) {
    v <- data[[nm]]
    grid[[nm]] <- if (is.numeric(v)) stats::median(v, na.rm = TRUE) else {
      tab <- table(v)
      names(tab)[which.max(tab)]
    }
  }
  grid$prob <- predict(model, new_data = grid, type = "prob")$.pred_Gentoo
  ggplot(data, aes(!!sym(f1), !!sym(f2))) +
    geom_raster(
      data = grid,
      aes(!!sym(f1), !!sym(f2), fill = prob),
      interpolate = TRUE,
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_point(aes(color = y), size = 2.2) +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "turquoise", high = "magenta", name = "P(Gentoo)") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(Adelie = "black", Gentoo = "white"), name = "Species") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = title, x = f1, y = f2)
}

save_day04_composite <- function(model, data, title, chunk_id, f1 = "bill_length_mm", f2 = "bill_depth_mm") {
  path <- paste0(knitr::fig_chunk(chunk_id), ".png")
  dir.create(dirname(path), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  if (requireNamespace("ragg", quietly = TRUE)) {
    ragg::agg_png(path, width = 10, height = 5, units = "in", res = 120)
  } else {
    png(path, width = 10, height = 5, units = "in", res = 120)
  }
  on.exit(dev.off(), add = TRUE)
  layout(matrix(1:2, nrow = 1, ncol = 2))
  par(mar = c(1, 1, 3, 1))
  if (inherits(model, "workflow")) {
    fit_obj <- extract_fit_parsnip(model)
    if (inherits(fit_obj$fit, "rpart")) {
      rpart.plot::rpart.plot(fit_obj$fit, type = 4, extra = 104, main = title, cex = 0.8)
    } else {
      plot(1, type = "n", xlab = "", ylab = "", main = title)
      text(1, 1, "Tree plot: rpart engine only", cex = 0.9)
    }
  } else {
    plot(1, type = "n", xlab = "", ylab = "", main = title)
    text(1, 1, "See boundary panel", cex = 0.9)
  }
  print(plot_prob_boundary(model, data, f1, f2))
  path
}
```

## Teaching scenarios (Part C extensions)

We deliberately stress the penguins in three ways:

```{r}
#| label: day04-scenarios-show
#| echo: false
#| message: false
dplyr::count(peng_imb, y, name = "n")
sum(is.na(peng_na$bill_length_mm))
ncol(peng_pca)
```

| Object | Stress | Recipe extension (Part C) |
|--------|--------|---------------------------|
| **`peng_imb`** | Rare Gentoo (~10 vs ~150 Adelie) | `step_upsample(y)`  -  when it helps |
| **`peng_na`** | Missing bill length and some `sex` | `step_impute_*()` |
| **`peng_pca`** | Extra size measures (correlated) | `step_pca()` |

# Part B  -  Many models (accuracy only) {#part-b-models}

**Rule:** keep **`add_recipe(rec_base)`** and **`folds`**  -  change **`add_model(spec)`** only.

## Model catalog and engines

**Same `rec_base` from Tuesday**  -  change **`spec`** (and `set_engine()`); keep **`workflow()`** + **`folds`**.

| Model spec | Typical tuned knobs | Common engines |
|------------|---------------------|----------------|
| `decision_tree()` | `tree_depth`, `min_n`, `cost_complexity` | `rpart`, `C5.0` |
| `rand_forest()` (**RF**) | `mtry`, `min_n`, `trees` | `ranger`, `randomForest` |
| `boost_tree()` (**GBM**) | `trees`, `tree_depth`, `learn_rate` | `xgboost`, `lightgbm`, `C5.0` |
| `logistic_reg()` | `penalty` | `glm`, `glmnet` |
| `linear_reg()` | `penalty` | `lm`, `glmnet` |
| `mlp()` (**MLP**) | `hidden_units`, `penalty`, `epochs` | `nnet`, `keras` |
| `svm_rbf()` (**SVM**) | `cost`, `rbf_sigma` | `kernlab` |
| `nearest_neighbor()` | `neighbors` | `kknn` |

## Model catalog  -  tuning and references

- **`mtry`**: number of predictors considered at each split (RF only).
- **`tune()`** marks hyperparameters for **`tune_grid()`**.
- Lookup: [tidymodels  -  find models](https://www.tidymodels.org/find/parsnip/) · [Engines at a glance](https://www.tidymodels.org/find/parsnip/#engines-at-a-glance).
- More depth: [Module 05](../modules/module-05-advanced-models.qmd) · [Module 08](../modules/module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd).

## Parsnip: type → engine → mode

Every spec needs: **model type** → **`set_engine()`** → **`set_mode("classification")`** (or regression).

```{r}
#| label: day04-catalog-dials
#| echo: true
#| message: false
rf_tune_spec <- rand_forest(trees = tune(), mtry = tune()) |>
  set_engine("ranger") |>
  set_mode("classification")
extract_parameter_set_dials(rf_tune_spec)
```

## Same recipe, swap the `spec`

Part A gave **`wf_tree`** (`rec_base` + `tree_spec`). Below: **random forest** (bagging) and **XGBoost** (boosting) on the same **`folds`**.

```{r}
#| label: day04-tree-boundary-recap
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
fit_tree_vis <- fit(wf_tree, peng_bal)
print(
  plot_prob_boundary(
    fit_tree_vis, peng_bal,
    title = "Tuesday's tree  -  rectangular regions in bill space"
  )
)
```

## Afternoon lab — Task 4.1

{{< include ../_includes/lab/d04-41.qmd >}}

## Bagging: random forests

- **Many trees** on **bootstrap** samples of rows; each split sees only a **random subset** of predictors → lower variance than one deep tree.
- **`rand_forest(mtry = ..., trees = ..., min_n = ...) |> set_engine("ranger")`**

## Boosting: gradient boosted trees

- Trees are added **sequentially**; each new tree targets **errors left by the ensemble** so far.
- **`boost_tree(trees = ..., tree_depth = ..., learn_rate = ...) |> set_engine("xgboost")`**
- **`learn_rate`**: shrink each tree's contribution (smaller → more trees needed, often smoother).

## Forest and boosting specs (code)

```{r}
#| label: day04-ensemble-specs
#| echo: true
#| message: false
rf_spec <- rand_forest(mtry = 3, trees = 400, min_n = 2) |>
  set_engine("ranger", importance = "impurity") |>
  set_mode("classification")
xgb_spec <- boost_tree(trees = 150, tree_depth = 3, learn_rate = 0.05) |>
  set_engine("xgboost") |>
  set_mode("classification")

list(tree = tree_spec, rf = rf_spec, xgb = xgb_spec)
```

```{r}
#| label: day04-ensemble-prep
#| echo: true
#| message: false
wf_rf <- workflow() |> add_recipe(rec_base) |> add_model(rf_spec)
wf_xgb <- workflow() |> add_recipe(rec_base) |> add_model(xgb_spec)
```

{{< include ../_includes/tidymodels-evaluation-workflow.qmd >}}

## Cross-validated accuracy (fit three specs)

```{r}
#| label: day04-ensemble-resamples
#| echo: true
#| message: false
set.seed(7)
rs_rf <- fit_resamples(wf_rf, folds, metrics = metrics_acc)
rs_xgb <- fit_resamples(wf_xgb, folds, metrics = metrics_acc)

cmp_ens <- bind_rows(
  collect_metrics(rs_tree) |> mutate(model = "Single tree"),
  collect_metrics(rs_rf) |> mutate(model = "Random forest"),
  collect_metrics(rs_xgb) |> mutate(model = "XGBoost")
) |>
  filter(.metric == "accuracy")
```

## Cross-validated accuracy (results table)

```{r}
#| label: day04-ensemble-resamples-table
#| echo: false
cmp_ens |>
  select(model, mean, std_err) |>
  arrange(desc(mean))
```

## Cross-validated accuracy (bar chart)

```{r}
#| label: day04-ensemble-auc-bar
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
cmp_ens |>
  ggplot(aes(reorder(model, mean), mean, fill = model)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - std_err, ymax = mean + std_err), width = 0.12) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = "Same folds, same recipe  -  different spec (accuracy)",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Mean accuracy"
  )
```

## Afternoon lab — Task 4.2

{{< include ../_includes/lab/d04-42.qmd >}}

Fixed settings here for speed  -  in projects, wrap each spec in **`tune_grid()`** like [Module 04](../modules/module-04-pipeline.qmd#canonical-pipeline-tuesday).

## Boosting: watch the boundary sharpen

We fix **`tree_depth = 2`** and **`learn_rate = 0.3`**, then increase **`trees`**. Each round adds small trees that correct leftover mistakes  -  the **Gentoo probability surface** becomes more **nonlinear**. We show rounds **1, 3, and 6** (the pattern is similar in between).

```{r}
#| label: day04-boost-spec-show
#| echo: true
#| message: false
boost_tree(trees = 6, tree_depth = 2, learn_rate = 0.3) |>
  set_engine("xgboost") |>
  set_mode("classification")
```

```{r}
#| label: day04-boost-boundary-prep
#| echo: false
#| results: hide
boost_trees <- c(1, 3, 6)
for (i in boost_trees) {
  spec_i <- boost_tree(
    trees = i,
    tree_depth = 2,
    learn_rate = 0.3
  ) |>
    set_engine("xgboost") |>
    set_mode("classification")
  wf_i <- workflow() |>
    add_recipe(rec_base) |>
    add_model(spec_i)
  assign(paste0("boost_fit_", i), fit(wf_i, peng_cls))
}
```

## Boosting round 1

```{r}
#| label: day04-boost-step-1
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
print(
  plot_prob_boundary(
    get("boost_fit_1"), peng_cls,
    title = "XGBoost after 1 tree (bill length × bill depth)"
  )
)
```

One shallow tree: rough split  -  mostly **underfits** the Gentoo cloud.

## Boosting round 3

```{r}
#| label: day04-boost-step-3
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
print(
  plot_prob_boundary(
    get("boost_fit_3"), peng_cls,
    title = "XGBoost after 3 trees (middle stage)"
  )
)
```

## Boosting round 6

```{r}
#| label: day04-boost-step-6
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
print(
  plot_prob_boundary(
    get("boost_fit_6"), peng_cls,
    title = "XGBoost after 6 trees"
  )
)
```

## Bagging vs boosting (same bill plane)

- **Bagging** (**RF**): many trees on bootstrap samples; predictions **average** → often **smoother** boundaries.
- **Boosting** (**XGBoost** / **GBM**): trees added **sequentially** to fix errors → can look **sharper** after several rounds (previous slides).

```{r}
#| label: day04-rf-boundary-prep
#| echo: false
#| results: hide
rf_boundary_fit <- fit(wf_rf, peng_bal)
```

## Random forest boundary (bagging)

```{r}
#| label: day04-rf-xgb-compare-rf
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
print(
  plot_prob_boundary(
    rf_boundary_fit, peng_bal,
    title = "Random forest (bagging)  -  averaged surface"
  )
)
```

## XGBoost boundary after 6 trees (boosting)

```{r}
#| label: day04-rf-xgb-compare-xgb
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
print(
  plot_prob_boundary(
    get("boost_fit_6"), peng_cls,
    title = "XGBoost after 6 trees  -  staged corrections"
  )
)
```

## Neural network (small MLP)  -  schematic

```{r}
#| label: day04-mlp-schematic
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 7
#| out-width: 100%
print(plot_mlp_schematic())
```

## Neural network (small MLP) 

- **MLP** = **multilayer perceptron**: one hidden layer of neurons; **`hidden_units`** sets its width.
- **`epochs`**: how many full passes through the training data.
- **`mlp(hidden_units = ..., penalty = ..., epochs = ...) |> set_engine("nnet")`**  -  weight decay via **`penalty`**.
- **Requires scaling** (already in `rec_base` via `step_normalize`).
- On **small tabular** data, trees often match or beat MLPs  -  still worth knowing the **same workflow** interface.

## MLP spec and workflow (code)

```{r}
#| label: day04-mlp-spec
#| echo: true
mlp_spec <- mlp(hidden_units = 8, penalty = 0.1, epochs = 150) |>
  set_engine("nnet", trace = FALSE) |>
  set_mode("classification")

wf_mlp <- workflow() |>
  add_recipe(rec_base) |>
  add_model(mlp_spec)
```

## MLP vs forests (cross-validated accuracy)

```{r}
#| label: day04-mlp-resamples
#| echo: true
#| message: false
set.seed(9)
rs_mlp <- fit_resamples(wf_mlp, folds, metrics = metrics_acc)
mlp_cmp <- bind_rows(
  collect_metrics(rs_rf) |> mutate(model = "Random forest"),
  collect_metrics(rs_xgb) |> mutate(model = "XGBoost"),
  collect_metrics(rs_mlp) |> mutate(model = "MLP (nnet)")
) |>
  filter(.metric == "accuracy")
```

```{r}
#| label: day04-mlp-resamples-table
#| echo: false
mlp_cmp |>
  select(model, .metric, mean, std_err)
```

```{r}
#| label: day04-mlp-bar
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
mlp_cmp |>
  ggplot(aes(model, mean, fill = model)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - std_err, ymax = mean + std_err), width = 0.12) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Same recipe  -  accuracy across specs", x = NULL, y = "Mean accuracy")
```

## MLP decision boundary (bill plane)

```{r}
#| label: day04-mlp-boundary-prep
#| echo: false
#| results: hide
fit_mlp_vis <- fit(wf_mlp, peng_bal)
```

```{r}
#| label: day04-mlp-boundary
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
print(
  plot_workflow_prob_boundary(
    fit_mlp_vis, peng_bal, "bill_length_mm", "bill_depth_mm",
    title = "MLP (nnet)  -  P(Gentoo) after fitting"
  )
)
```

## Logistic regression boundary (bill plane)

```{r}
#| label: day04-glm-boundary
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
glm_spec <- logistic_reg() |>
  set_engine("glm") |>
  set_mode("classification")
wf_glm <- workflow() |>
  add_recipe(rec_base) |>
  add_model(glm_spec)
fit_glm_peng <- fit(wf_glm, peng_bal)
print(
  plot_workflow_prob_boundary(
    fit_glm_peng, peng_bal, "bill_length_mm", "bill_depth_mm",
    title = "Logistic regression  -  smooth linear boundary"
  )
)
```

Full three-way comparison: [Module 08](../modules/module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd).

## Bridge: from model choice to feature engineering

Same workflow, new questions:

- **Part B:** which **model family** fits best?
- **Part C:** can **PCA inside the recipe** help?
- **Part D:** how do we handle **missing values** in the recipe?
- **Parts E–F:** which **metrics** and **interpretation** tools matter when classes are imbalanced?

# Part C  -  Dimension reduction (accuracy only) {#part-c-pca}

## Extension: principal component analysis (PCA) in the recipe

**PCA** combines correlated numeric predictors into orthogonal **principal components**  -  fit **`step_pca()`** on training folds only, same as `step_normalize()`.

## PCA motivation: correlated measurements

For PCA we add back **flipper length** and **body mass**  -  bills and size track together on penguins.

```{r}
#| label: day04-pca-corr
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
num_cols <- c("bill_length_mm", "bill_depth_mm", "flipper_length_mm", "body_mass_g", "year")
cor_pca <- cor(peng_pca[, num_cols])
cor_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(cor_pca)) |>
  dplyr::rename(var1 = Var1, var2 = Var2, r = Freq)
ggplot(cor_df, aes(var1, var2, fill = r)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "steelblue", mid = "white", high = "firebrick", midpoint = 0, limit = c(-1, 1)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  coord_fixed() +
  labs(title = "Correlated morphometrics (why PCA?)", fill = "r")
```

## Recipe with `step_pca()` (code)

```{r}
#| label: day04-pca-recipe-code
#| echo: true
rec_pca <- recipe(y ~ ., data = peng_pca) |>
  step_zv(all_predictors()) |>
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) |>
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) |>
  step_pca(all_numeric_predictors(), num_comp = 2)
```

Steps: zero-variance removal → dummies → scale numerics → **retain 2 principal components**.

## Variance explained by each component

```{r}
#| label: day04-pca-variance
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
num_scaled <- peng_pca |>
  dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(num_cols)) |>
  scale()
pc_fit <- prcomp(num_scaled, center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE)
var_pct <- pc_fit$sdev^2 / sum(pc_fit$sdev^2) * 100
var_df <- tibble(PC = paste0("PC", seq_along(var_pct)), variance_pct = var_pct)
ggplot(var_df, aes(PC, variance_pct)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Variance explained (same numerics as step_pca)",
    x = NULL,
    y = "% variance"
  )
```

## PCA biplot  -  scores vs loadings

- **Points** = **scores**: where each penguin sits in **PC1-PC2** space (compressed view of all numerics).
- **Arrows** = **loadings** (preview): which original measurements push in which direction  -  detail on the next slides.

```{r}
#| label: day04-pca-biplot
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
print(plot_pca_biplot(peng_pca, num_cols, y_col = "y"))
```

## What are PCA loadings?

After **`step_normalize()`**, each component is a **weighted sum** of the scaled columns:

$$\text{PC1} = w_{11}\,\text{bill\_length} + w_{12}\,\text{bill\_depth} + \cdots$$

- **Loadings** = those weights $w_{jk}$ (stored in the **`rotation`** matrix from **`prcomp()`** / **`step_pca()`**).
- **Sign:** positive loading → birds **above average** on that measurement tend to have **higher** scores on that PC.
- **Magnitude:** larger $|\text{loading}|$ → that measurement matters **more** for that component (after scaling).

**Scores** tell you where a penguin is; **loadings** tell you **which measurements built** that axis.

## Loadings on PC1 and PC2 (bar chart)

Each bar = one original column's weight on that component (predictors were **scaled** before PCA).

```{r}
#| label: day04-pca-loadings-bars
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
print(plot_pca_loadings_bars(peng_pca, num_cols))
```

## How to read these loadings (penguins)

```{r}
#| label: day04-pca-loadings-table
#| echo: false
num_scaled <- peng_pca |>
  dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(num_cols)) |>
  scale()
load_tbl <- as.data.frame(round(prcomp(num_scaled, center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE)$rotation[, 1:2], 2))
load_tbl$measurement <- rownames(load_tbl)
load_tbl <- load_tbl |> dplyr::select(measurement, PC1, PC2)
knitr::kable(load_tbl, caption = "Loadings (rounded)  -  same numerics as the plot above")
```

- **PC1** loads heavily on **bill length, flipper length, body mass** (and bill depth negatively) → a **"overall size"** axis: big birds vs small birds along PC1.
- **PC2** is dominated by **year** → mostly **study year**, not morphology (weak for separating species here).
- **Takeaway:** PCA merged correlated size measures into **PC1**; the model later sees **PC1/PC2**, not "mm of bill" directly  -  harder to explain biologically than raw columns.

## XGBoost with PCA vs without (same folds)

```{r}
#| label: day04-pca-prep-models
#| echo: false
#| results: hide
xgb_pca_spec <- boost_tree(trees = 100, tree_depth = 3, learn_rate = 0.05) |>
  set_engine("xgboost") |>
  set_mode("classification")

rec_no_pca <- recipe(y ~ ., data = peng_pca) |>
  step_zv(all_predictors()) |>
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) |>
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors())

wf_pca <- workflow() |> add_recipe(rec_pca) |> add_model(xgb_pca_spec)
wf_no_pca <- workflow() |> add_recipe(rec_no_pca) |> add_model(xgb_pca_spec)
```

```{r}
#| label: day04-pca-fit-compare
#| echo: true
#| message: false
set.seed(8)
rs_pca <- fit_resamples(wf_pca, folds_pca, metrics = metrics_acc)
set.seed(8)
rs_no_pca <- fit_resamples(wf_no_pca, folds_pca, metrics = metrics_acc)
```

## XGBoost with PCA vs without (accuracy table)

```{r}
#| label: day04-pca-fit-compare-table
#| echo: false
bind_rows(
  collect_metrics(rs_pca) |> mutate(model = "XGBoost + PCA (2 comp.)"),
  collect_metrics(rs_no_pca) |> mutate(model = "XGBoost, no PCA")
) |>
  filter(.metric == "accuracy") |>
  select(model, mean, std_err)
```

## XGBoost with PCA vs without (bar chart)

```{r}
#| label: day04-pca-auc-bar
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
bind_rows(
  collect_metrics(rs_pca) |> mutate(model = "With PCA"),
  collect_metrics(rs_no_pca) |> mutate(model = "No PCA")
) |>
  filter(.metric == "accuracy") |>
  ggplot(aes(model, mean, fill = model)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - std_err, ymax = mean + std_err), width = 0.12) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(title = "Accuracy: PCA vs all scaled numerics", x = NULL, y = "Mean accuracy")
```

## PCA tradeoffs and leakage

- **Tradeoff:** components are **not** "bill mm" anymore  -  **VIP** on PCA columns is harder to explain biologically.
- **Leakage check:** always fit PCA **inside** `workflow()` + resampling, never on the full table before splitting.

## Bridge: from PCA to missing data

Same workflow, new recipe stress: we now handle **missing values** with imputation steps **inside** cross-validation.

# Part D  -  Missing data (accuracy only) {#part-d-impute}

## Extension: imputation inside the recipe (`peng_na`)

Add **`step_impute_*()`** before `step_zv()` / dummies / normalize  -  never `drop_na()` on the full table before splitting.

## Artificial missing data: bill length

We set **~15%** of `bill_length_mm` (and a few `sex`) to **NA** (**missing** values  -  "not available") to practice imputation  -  not a real sensor failure pattern.

```{r}
#| label: day04-na-scatter
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
plot_na_highlight(
  peng_na, "bill_length_mm", "bill_depth_mm",
  title = "Missing bill length (red ×)"
)
```

## Wrong fix: drop rows with `drop_na()`

```{r}
#| label: day04-na-drop-demo
#| echo: true
tibble(
  rows_before = nrow(peng_na),
  rows_after_drop_na = nrow(tidyr::drop_na(peng_na))
)
```

Dropping loses penguins and can **bias** who remains  -  never drop on the **full** table before splitting.

## Right fix: impute inside the recipe

- **`step_impute_median()`** for numeric **`bill_length_mm`**; **`step_impute_mode()`** for categorical **`sex`**.
- Then the usual **`step_zv` → `step_dummy` → `step_normalize`** chain from Part A.

```{r}
#| label: day04-impute-recipe
#| echo: true
rec_impute <- recipe(y ~ ., data = peng_na) |>
  step_impute_median(bill_length_mm) |>
  step_impute_mode(sex) |>
  step_zv(all_predictors()) |>
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) |>
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors())
```

## Before imputation

Rows with **missing bill length** sit in the **left margin** (pink band)  -  we do **not** know their x-position yet.

```{r}
#| label: day04-impute-prep-plots
#| echo: false
#| results: hide
prep_impute <- prep(rec_impute, peng_na)
rec_impute_fill <- recipe(y ~ ., data = peng_na) |>
  step_impute_median(bill_length_mm) |>
  step_impute_mode(sex)
peng_filled <- bake(prep(rec_impute_fill, peng_na), peng_na)
peng_na_plot <- peng_na |>
  mutate(bill_length_true = bill_length_true, row_id = dplyr::row_number())
```

```{r}
#| label: day04-impute-before
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
print(
  plot_na_highlight(
    peng_na, "bill_length_mm", "bill_depth_mm",
    title = "Before imputation  -  missing bill length"
  )
)
```

## After imputation

**Open circles** = true bill length (simulation only). **Gold** = median imputed value for the **same** penguin. **Orange arrows** connect true → imputed. Gray points are other birds (observed bill length).

```{r}
#| label: day04-impute-after
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
print(
  plot_imputed_highlight(
    peng_na_plot,
    peng_filled,
    "bill_length_mm",
    "bill_depth_mm",
    true_xvar = "bill_length_true",
    title = "After median imputation  -  bill length filled in"
  )
)
```

```{r}
#| label: day04-impute-na-count
#| echo: true
sum(is.na(bake(prep_impute, peng_na)$bill_length_mm))
```

## Imputation inside cross-validation

```{r}
#| label: day04-impute-cv
#| echo: true
#| message: false
xgb_na_spec <- boost_tree(trees = 80, tree_depth = 3, learn_rate = 0.08) |>
  set_engine("xgboost") |>
  set_mode("classification")
wf_na <- workflow() |>
  add_recipe(rec_impute) |>
  add_model(xgb_na_spec)
folds_na <- vfold_cv(peng_na, v = 5, strata = y)
set.seed(14)
rs_na <- fit_resamples(wf_na, folds_na, metrics = metrics_acc)
collect_metrics(rs_na) |>
  filter(.metric == "accuracy") |>
  select(mean, std_err)
```

The workflow accepts **NA** in raw rows; each fold **learns** imputation on analysis data only.

## Metrics and deployment (reminders)

- Pick **metrics before** peeking at holdout data; report **subgroup** tables when ethically appropriate.
- **Data card:** document NA handling and class prevalence **before** modeling.
- **Model card:** intended use, limits, monitoring  -  finish the draft you started Monday.

Continue: [Module 06  -  Metrics & SHAP](../modules/module-06-evaluation-and-interpretability.qmd) · [Day 2 (Tuesday) pipeline](../slides/day-02-tuesday.qmd).

# Part E  -  Imbalance, metrics, and confusion matrices {#part-e-metrics}

## Bridge: from binary metrics to multiclass imbalance

We move from Adelie vs Gentoo to **three species**, with a deliberately rare **Chinstrap** class.
Accuracy alone is no longer enough — we add **macro recall/F1** and **per-class recall**.

## Multiclass imbalance (`peng_imb3`)

We now use **three-species classification** (Adelie, Gentoo, Chinstrap).
For a clean imbalance lesson, we keep only **15 Chinstrap** rows (deterministic selection), while Adelie and Gentoo stay abundant.

```{r}
#| label: day04-imb3-balance-plot
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
peng_imb3 |>
  dplyr::count(y3) |>
  ggplot(aes(y3, n, fill = y3)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.25, size = 4) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = "Manufactured multiclass imbalance (rare Chinstrap)",
    x = "Species",
    y = "n"
  )
```

## Why accuracy alone can still mislead

```{r}
#| label: day04-imb3-majority
#| echo: true
#| message: false
majority_label <- peng_imb3 |>
  dplyr::count(y3, sort = TRUE) |>
  dplyr::slice(1) |>
  dplyr::pull(y3) |>
  as.character()

tibble(
  strategy = paste("Always predict", majority_label),
  accuracy_on_peng_imb3 = mean(as.character(peng_imb3$y3) == majority_label)
)
```

- Overall accuracy can look fine while one class (here **Chinstrap**) is mostly missed.
- So we track **macro recall**, **macro F1**, and **per-class recall**.

## Recipes and model (`peng_imb3`)

```{r}
#| label: day04-imb3-recipes
#| echo: true
#| message: false
mc_tree_spec <- decision_tree(tree_depth = 4, min_n = 20) |>
  set_engine("rpart") |>
  set_mode("classification")

rec_imb3 <- recipe(y3 ~ ., data = peng_imb3) |>
  step_zv(all_predictors()) |>
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) |>
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors())

rec_imb3_up <- recipe(y3 ~ ., data = peng_imb3) |>
  step_upsample(y3) |>
  step_zv(all_predictors()) |>
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) |>
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors())

wf_imb3 <- workflow() |> add_recipe(rec_imb3) |> add_model(mc_tree_spec)
wf_imb3_up <- workflow() |> add_recipe(rec_imb3_up) |> add_model(mc_tree_spec)
```

## Cross-validated metrics (multiclass)

```{r}
#| label: day04-imb3-cv-fits
#| echo: true
#| message: false
set.seed(11)
rs_imb3 <- fit_resamples(wf_imb3, folds_imb3, metrics = metrics_cls_imb3)
set.seed(12)
rs_imb3_up <- fit_resamples(wf_imb3_up, folds_imb3, metrics = metrics_cls_imb3)

compare_metrics_tbl_multiclass(
  "No upsample" = rs_imb3,
  "Upsample" = rs_imb3_up
)
```

## Holdout confusion matrix: no upsample

```{r}
#| label: day04-imb3-holdout-no
#| echo: true
#| message: false
set.seed(13)
split_imb3 <- initial_split(peng_imb3, prop = 0.8, strata = y3)
fit_imb3 <- fit(wf_imb3, training(split_imb3))
pred_imb3 <- augment(fit_imb3, testing(split_imb3))

pred_imb3 |>
  conf_mat(truth = y3, estimate = .pred_class)
```

## Holdout confusion matrix: with upsample

```{r}
#| label: day04-imb3-holdout-up
#| echo: true
#| message: false
fit_imb3_up <- fit(wf_imb3_up, training(split_imb3))
pred_imb3_up <- augment(fit_imb3_up, testing(split_imb3))

pred_imb3_up |>
  conf_mat(truth = y3, estimate = .pred_class)
```

## Per-class recall (holdout)

```{r}
#| label: day04-imb3-recall-compare
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 5
#| out-width: 100%
plot_multiclass_recall_compare(pred_imb3, pred_imb3_up, highlight_class = "Chinstrap")
```

- In the confusion matrix: **rows = true species**, **columns = predicted species**.
- Upsampling is not a free lunch, but it often improves **minority-class recall** on this engineered imbalance.
- Use macro/per-class metrics when one class is underrepresented.

# Part F  -  Variable importance {#part-f-vip}

## Bridge: from metrics to interpretation

After choosing metrics for imbalance, we ask a different question: **which predictors drove the fitted model?** (VIP and SHAP in the next section.)

## Data the forest sees (bill plane)

```{r}
#| label: day04-vip-scatter
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
ggplot(peng_bal, aes(bill_length_mm, bill_depth_mm, color = y)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.6) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(Adelie = "turquoise", Gentoo = "magenta")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Adelie vs Gentoo in bill space",
    x = "Bill length (mm)",
    y = "Bill depth (mm)"
  )
```

## Variable importance (VIP)

- **VIP** = **variable importance plot**  -  how often splits use each **baked** predictor (impurity in **`ranger`**).
- **Correlated** bill measures can **share** importance  -  not proof that changing one bill mm **causes** species shift.

```{r}
#| label: day04-vip-prep
#| echo: false
#| results: hide
rf_fit <- fit(wf_rf, peng_cls)
```

## Variable importance plot

```{r}
#| label: day04-vip-plot
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 100%
rf_fit |>
  extract_fit_parsnip() |>
  vip(geom = "point", num_features = 12) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = "Variable importance (random forest)",
    subtitle = "Adelie vs Gentoo  -  descriptive ranking, not causal"
  )
```

## Afternoon lab — Task 4.3

{{< include ../_includes/lab/d04-43.qmd >}}

## Permutation importance (pointer)

- **Permutation importance**: shuffle one column, watch metric drop  -  model-agnostic; see [Module 06](../modules/module-06-evaluation-and-interpretability.qmd).

{{< include ../_includes/day04-shap-sex.qmd >}}

# Part G  -  Wrap-up

## Afternoon lab — Task 4.5

{{< include ../_includes/lab/d04-45.qmd >}}

## Nested resampling (when tuning gets serious)

- **Nested CV**: an **outer** cross-validation loop estimates performance; an **inner** loop tunes hyperparameters inside each outer training fold.
- Avoid tuning and scoring on the **same** held-out rows (optimistic bias).

## End-of-day checklist

- Name three **`parsnip` model types** and a valid **engine** for each
- Why must **`step_pca()`** and **`step_impute_*()`** live **inside** the recipe?
- When might **`step_upsample()`** not improve CV scores?
- Which metric would you report for a **rare-event screening** study?
- One reason **VIP** on correlated bills is **not** a causal claim
- One sentence you would **not** say about a SHAP plot on penguin sex
